description_long: This cowshed features a large, low-pitched hip roof clad in reddish-brown shingles, providing ample coverage and weather protection. The structure is primarily constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. One side of the building is partially open, supported by thick wooden posts, likely serving as an entry or sheltered area for livestock. The remaining sides are fully enclosed, with no visible windows, emphasizing security and containment. The roof overhangs the walls, offering additional shade and rain protection. The overall design is functional and robust, typical of agricultural outbuildings found on the outskirts of urban environments.
description: A large, wooden cowshed with a reddish hip roof, partially open on one side and fully enclosed on the others, designed for livestock shelter.
description_view_0: This view shows the cowshed's partially open side with wooden posts and the expansive hip roof.
description_view_1: This view displays the narrow end of the cowshed, fully enclosed with no visible openings.
description_view_2: This view highlights the long, fully enclosed wooden wall beneath the hip roof, with no visible doors or windows.
description_view_3: This view shows the opposite narrow end, also fully enclosed, with the roof's overhang clearly visible.
attribute_door_style: open bay with wooden support posts, no visible hinged or sliding doors
